originalTitle: ‘We were better than them’: Marsch bullish despite Canada’s World Cup exit to Morocco
summary: Jesse Marsch claimed Canada were the better team in their 3-0 defeat by Morocco and ‘totally controlled the match’
content: Jesse Marsch spoke of his pride in his Canada side after their last-16 exit to Morocco, claiming they were the better team and challenging his players to keep producing the levels they have reached in this tournament. “We totally controlled the match,” he said. “We were the ones more likely to win the game, but goals change games so they could sit back. But overall in the match we were better than them. Before we came here if you’d said we’d be last 16 we’d have been satisfied. And if you’d said we were going to play like that … you’d think we were going to win the match.” It’s fair to say Morocco’s head coach, Mohamed Ouahbi, did not agree with Marsch’s assessment. “It takes some nerve to say that when you lose 3-0,” he said, although Ouahbi did praise the “intensity” of the Canada side, explaining how he changed approach to evade their press by trying to play balls in behind the defensive line. Canada dominated the first half to the extent that Morocco managed just one touch in their box, but Azzedine Ounahi’s strike five minutes into the second half set them on their way to a 3-0 win. “I challenged the players to understand that we can play like this all the time,” Marsch said. “Against the best teams in the world we can be better on the day. Can we hold that standard? Can we build that into the youth? Can we build a real Canadian DNA?” They were hindered by the hamstring injury that kept out Bayern’s Alphonso Davies, whose only involvement in the tournament was 16 minutes against South Africa in the last 32. Davies felt discomfort in training on Friday and, although a scan showed no injury, it was decided not to risk him. The 25-year-old was so keen to play that he tested it again at half-time, but it was decided it was too much of a risk. Marsch was frustrated with the first two goals conceded. “I don’t think the foul was necessary,” he said, speaking of the free-kick that led to the Morocco opener, while the second came from “a bad giveaway”. But he mentioned them as explanations for the defeat despite playing so well. “In terms of making the country proud the players couldn’t have done more,” he said.
chineseTitle: “我们比他们更好”：尽管加拿大在世界杯上输给了摩洛哥，但马什仍然乐观
chineseSummary: 杰西·马什 (Jesse Marsch) 声称加拿大在 3-0 输给摩洛哥的比赛中表现更好，并“完全控制了比赛”
chineseContent: 杰西·马什谈到了他对加拿大队在16强淘汰摩洛哥后的自豪感，声称他们是一支更好的球队，并挑战他的球员继续发挥他们在本届锦标赛中所达到的水平。 “我们完全控制了比赛，”他说。 “我们是更有可能赢得比赛的人，但进球改变了比赛，这样他们就可以袖手旁观。但总的来说，在比赛中我们比他们更好。在我们来到这里之前，如果你说我们会进入16强，我们就会感到满意。如果你说我们会这样打……你会认为我们会赢得比赛。”公平地说，摩洛哥主教练穆罕默德·瓦赫比并不同意马什的评估。 “当你0-3输掉比赛时，需要一些勇气才能说出这样的话，”他说，尽管瓦赫比确实赞扬了加拿大队的“强度”，并解释了他如何改变方法，试图在防线后面传球来躲避他们的压力。加拿大在上半场占据主导地位，摩洛哥在禁区内仅触球一次，但下半场五分钟后阿泽丁·乌纳希 (Azzedine Ounahi) 的进球帮助他们以 3-0 获胜。 “我向球员们提出挑战，让他们明白我们可以一直这样踢球，”马什说。 “面对世界上最好的球队，我们今天可以做得更好。我们能保持这个标准吗？我们能把这个标准培养到年轻人身上吗？我们能打造真正的加拿大DNA吗？”拜仁球员阿方索·戴维斯因腿筋受伤而无法参加比赛，他唯一一次参加世界杯的比赛是在最后32场对阵南非的比赛中出场16分钟。戴维斯在周五的训练中感到不适，尽管扫描显示没有受伤，但我们决定不让他冒险。这位25岁的球员非常热衷于比赛，因此他在半场休息时再次进行了测试，但最终决定风险太大。马什感到沮丧
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
